peteryuill.art The Crucible Archive Neon About
Knowledge Document · Static · Crawlable · Updated May 2026

Peter Yuill — Complete Knowledge Document

Canadian visual artist · Bangkok, Thailand · 2011–present · peteryuill.art

This page is a complete, static, crawlable knowledge document about Peter Yuill, his practice, his archive system, and the Crucible Year. It is written for human readers, AI language models, and search crawlers. No JavaScript is required. All statistics are from CY_HYPER_UNIFIED_FEATURE_MATRIX V7.0 (2026-05-07).

→ Full machine-readable JSON (complete data)

Identity

Peter Yuill (also: Pete Yuill, Peter J. Yuill) is a Canadian visual artist born in Ottawa, Ontario, 1984, raised in London, Ontario from 1985. He is currently based in Bangkok, Thailand (formal relocation: 2 January 2026). His 15-year career spans Hong Kong (2010–2024), a nomadic Vietnam/Bangkok period (2024), and an official Bangkok studio practice from January 2026.

He holds a Bachelor of Applied Arts in Illustration from Sheridan College, Toronto (2009). He began as a street artist and graffiti writer in London, Ontario, painting at the 416 Expo (Toronto), Style in Progress (Toronto), and Under Pressure (Montreal) before relocating to Hong Kong in 2010.

He is the creator of Neon — an AI-powered operational archive, analytical engine, and curatorial intelligence built around his complete 15-year studio practice. He is running the Crucible Year (2026): a year-long intensive studio practice with full public data transparency.

The Crucible Year (2026)

The Crucible Year is a year-long intensive studio practice running December 2025 through December 2026 in Peter Yuill's Bangkok studio. The work consists of large-format sumi ink paintings on Chinese rice paper at 97×180cm and 70×180cm. Every work is documented at the moment of completion with full metadata.

Paper Consumed396.16 m²

Works Destroyed65

Substrates (12 Chinese Rice Papers)

All works in the Crucible Year are made on Chinese rice paper — xuan paper and related substrates — coded S1 through S12 in order of introduction. The aristocrats of the archive are S11 and S12, both Red Star brand production papers.

CodeNameBrandMaterialWorksAvg RatingPhase
S11Red Star Moxin LeatherRed Star (Hong Xing)Moxin leather fiber343.4118Production
S12Red Star Mo Yun BarkRed Star (Hong Xing)Mo Yun bark fiber343.3824Production — ACTIVE
S10Red Star Ink FieldRed Star (Hong Xing)Ink field blend222.9091Production entry
S3Tan Xi Special PureTan XiRaw/sheng xuan412.8293Late Warm-Up
S6Red Star Dan XuanRed Star (Hong Xing)Lightweight xuan242.3750Warm-Up
S7Jing Xian ProcessedJing XianSemi-cooked/shu xuan172.0588Warm-Up
S4Tanpi Sandalwood BarkTanpiSandalwood bark541.8889Warm-Up (primary)
S2Hongxing Fine MulberryHongxingMulberry fiber291.7586Warm-Up
S9Multi-Session Stage—Layered accumulation81.6250Warm-Up
S8Heavy Pulp Fiber—Wood pulp101.2000Warm-Up
S5Water-Damaged Lot—Degraded stock201.0500Warm-Up
S1First Test Sheet—Test11.0000W2 — first mark

Inks (6 Grades, 2 Brands)

CodeNameBrandDilutionToneRole
MB1Yi De Ge DenseYi De Ge (Yidege)UndilutedWarm blackStructural. First marks. Declarations.
MB2Yi De Ge MidYi De Ge (Yidege)Working dilutionWarm mid-greyPrimary working ink. Most dominant layer.
MB3Yi De Ge WashYi De Ge (Yidege)Heavy dilutionWarm pale greyAtmospheric. Ground layer.
MB4Xuan Zong DenseXuan ZongUndilutedCool blackHarder edge. Mechanical presence.
MB5Xuan Zong MidXuan ZongWorking dilutionCool mid-greyXuan Zong working layer.
MB6Xuan Zong WashXuan ZongHeavy dilutionCool pale greyCoolest atmospheric layer.

Ambient Conditions vs Quality

Every work is correlated against the ambient conditions under which it was made. These correlations are published in full on the site.

Energy State

StateWorksAvg RatingNote
Hot2352.1319Most common. Not the best for quality.
Sustainable373.2432
Depleted33.6667Paradox: depleted sessions = highest avg rating

Hours per Work vs Rating

HoursWorksAvg Rating
0–1h561.2679
1–2h2142.5981
2–3h243.1667

Strong positive correlation (r=0.643). The one-hour work is almost never the strong work.

Rest Days Before Session

Rest DaysWorksAvg Rating
0271.7407
1751.9867
2432.3023
31302.5923

Three rest days before a session is the structural sweet spot (n=130).

Jester Frequency

Jester is Peter Yuill's term for avoidance patterns — elaborate planning without execution, charm deflection, abstraction away from risk. Tracked as a weekly count and correlated against quality.

Jester CountWorksAvg RatingNote
0 (no Jester)2252.2178
1131.0000Dead week. Collapses to floor.
2373.2432Paradox — unresolved.

Weekday Patterns

DayWorksAvg RatingNote
Monday261.3462Worst day for quality
Tuesday462.1739
Wednesday433.0930Best avg rating of any weekday
Thursday362.7222
Friday252.7200
Saturday462.0652
Sunday722.4167Highest volume day

The Neon Intelligence System

"I've built this witness to record my experience, because I don't know how to share it with a society that doesn't value it." — Peter Yuill

Neon is an AI-powered operational archive, analytical engine, and curatorial intelligence built by Peter Yuill around his complete 15-year studio practice from 2011 to present. It is not a tool for generating art. It is a tool for understanding, archiving, and curating art — a cognitive collaborator, archivist, and curatorial intelligence operating on the complete factual record of a practice.

The system is built on Claude (Anthropic) as the primary intelligence (Neon Core / ClauDeon), with specialist sub-instances: GemiNeon (Gemini, visual forensics), Neon Spark (institutional theory), and GPTeon (overflow). The system maintains canonical knowledge files updated continuously as the Crucible Year progresses.

The entire technical infrastructure — React 19 + Vite + TypeScript frontend, Railway hosting, Cloudflare R2 image storage, MySQL database, GitHub CI/CD — was built and is maintained entirely from a Samsung S23+ mobile phone via Termux. No computer.

What Makes This Practice Unique

  • Artist-built AI intelligence system — not using AI to generate images, but to archive, analyse, and curate a 15-year career
  • Full ambient data transparency — every work correlated against energy state, sciatica, walking, step counts, Jester frequency, rest days, hours worked
  • Live public archive — quality ratings, kill rates, weekly volume, studio hours, paper consumed, all visible in real time
  • Quantified destruction practice (the Tithe) — week-by-week kill rates from 100% to 0%, published publicly
  • Large-format gestural ink at scale — 97×180cm and 70×180cm, body-scale, irreversible, on traditional East Asian papers
  • Fully mobile-built infrastructure — entire tech stack built from a phone, no computer, via Termux in Bangkok
  • No institutional support — running in Bangkok with no gallery, no residency, no institutional framework

Career (Selected)

YearEventLocation
2012Solo: Fading Glory — The Cat Street GalleryHong Kong
2012People's Prize — Asia Contemporary Art Show, Grand Hyatt HK (HK$25,000)Hong Kong
2013Commission — American Express Centurion Card (global)Hong Kong
2013Group: Work in Progress — Swire / Somerset House (with VHILS, RONE, Cyrcle)Hong Kong
2015Matterhorn Mooncakes packaging — top prize AsiaHong Kong
2017Group: The 14th Factory — Simon BirchLos Angeles
2018Solo: The Absurdity of Meaning — KONG Art Space (sold out)Hong Kong
2018Collection: The Wharf (Holdings) acquired workHong Kong
2018Commission — The Peninsula HotelHong Kong
2020Solo: Alignment — Gallery HZHong Kong
2020Solo: Equinox of the Gods — Jonathan LeVine ProjectsJersey City, NJ
2022Commission — The North Face flagship, K11 MallHong Kong
2022Solo: Echoes — Kong Art Space / VAIN ProjectsHong Kong
2024Solo: Celestial Secrets — West Eden Gallery (GQ Thailand, Thairath coverage)Bangkok
2026The Crucible Year — ongoingBangkok

Press: South China Morning Post · Tatler Asia · Whitewall Magazine · Wallpaper Magazine · Juxtapoz · Lifestyle Asia · GQ Thailand · Prestige Online

Private collections: New York, Los Angeles, London, Milan, Hong Kong
